risinfo.chaptertitle: 21. Remote Data Collection and Monitoring Services

sect1.title: CHAPTER 21. REMOTE DATA COLLECTION AND MONITORING SERVICES

table.title: CHAPTER 21. REMOTE DATA COLLECTION AND MONITORING SERVICES

row.entry: Clinical staff or physician work • Patient education/training on self-measurement and reading collection • Calibration of home blood pressure monitoring device Report once per device.

Physician work • Review the report of individual and average systolic/diastolic blood pressure readings (it must include ≥12 readings; the patient performs/documents 2 readings 1 minute apart at each measurement). • Communicate the treatment plan to clinical staff or the patient/caregiver. Report once per calendar month.

sect1.para: Teaching Point: At least 12 blood pressure readings must be obtained and averaged to create a treatment plan for the patient. Code 99474 includes collection, analysis, and communication of treatment plan(s) within a 30-day period.

If the patient returned for an office or other outpatient E/M service to review the readings and obtain the physician's recommended care plan, an E/M service (eg, 99214) would be reported in lieu of 99474.

listitem.para: indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; ✱, add-on; ✱, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.

sect4.title: Nonspecific Physiologic Data Collection and Interpretation
sect4.para: 99091 Collection and interpretation of physiologic data (eg, electrocardiography, blood pressure, glucose monitoring) digitally stored and/or transmitted by the patient and/or caregiver to the physician or other QHP, qualified by education, training, licensure/regulation (when applicable) requiring a minimum of 30 minutes of time, each 30 days (1.10 work RVUs, 1.60 total RVUs in a facility or non-facility setting) Code 99091 does not require active monitoring of physiologic data or live interactive communication with the patient during the service period as required for remote physiologic monitoring treatment management service codes 99470, 99457, and 99458, discussed in the Remote Physiologic Monitoring Treatment Management Services section later in this chapter.
Code 99091 represents a 30-day episode of care. Time spent by a physician or QHP in the following activities is included in the time supporting 99091:

listitem.para: Modifying the care plan as necessary and communicating to the patient/caregiver
Report 99091 for a minimum of 30 minutes (cumulative time), each 30 days.

sect4.para: If the services described by 99091 are provided on the same day that the patient presents for an E/M service, these services should be considered part of the E/M service and not separately reported.
Do not report 99091 ✱ If other, more specific CPT codes exist (eg, 93227, 93272 for electrocardiographic services; 95251 for continuous glucose monitoring)
✱ For time spent by clinical staff ✱ For transfer and interpretation of data from hospital or clinical laboratory computers

✱ For time used to meet the criteria for care plan oversight services (99374–99380), personally performed CCM (99437, 99491) or PCM (99424–99427), or remote physiologic monitoring treatment management services (99457, 99458) CPT does not require use of a medical device as defined by the FDA for services reported with 99091. Live interactive contact during the reporting period is also not a required element of service for 99091.

▶ An adolescent with cystic fibrosis transmits physiologic data (self-reported and data from a combination of FDA-approved and non–FDA-approved medical devices) to their physician. The data includes results from a hand-held spirometry device and a Bluetooth oximeter, wellness and cough scores, temperature, weight, and smartwatch measurements of heart rate, exercise, and sleep. A care coordinator in the physician's practice receives the patient's data and notifies the physician of symptoms/trends that are outside the acceptable range designated by the physician. Over a 30-day period, the physician documents 30 minutes spent in review of the patient's data, updating the patient's plan of care, and providing instructions to the patient and patient's parents (as indicated). The physician reports 99091 linked to a diagnosis code for the current state of the child's cystic fibrosis (eg, E84.0, cystic fibrosis with pulmonary manifestations).
